title: The old man who repaired umbrellas
summary: A portrait of a fading craft seen on a small street in Hanoi.
topic: The old man who repaired umbrellas
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a craftsperson with a small old-fashioned job. Describe what they do and how their work feels different from modern life. Use past simple and 'used to'.
hintStarters: Near the corner of … and …, there was a man who…
Most people bought new umbrellas, but he…
While he was working on a broken umbrella, he…
sampleText: Near a quiet corner in the Old Quarter of Hanoi, there was a man who repaired umbrellas. I walked past him every morning on my way to a Vietnamese class. He sat on a small wooden stool with two boxes in front of him: one full of bent metal spokes and one full of dark, folded fabric. He did not have a shop sign. People who knew him simply put their broken umbrella on the ground next to him and asked the price. He used to charge twenty thousand đồng to replace a spoke, and forty thousand to sew the fabric. While he was working on a broken umbrella one afternoon, I asked him how long he had been doing this job. He laughed and said, 'Longer than you have been alive.' Most people in the city threw old umbrellas away and bought new ones for the price of two coffees. He told me he understood, but he did not want to do something else, because his fingers still knew the work. After the monsoon season ended that year, his corner was empty. A small woman selling flowers told me he had moved to live with his daughter outside the city.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một người đàn ông sửa ô dù
một chiếc ghế đẩu gỗ nhỏ
những nan kim loại bị cong

sampleAnnotations.phrase: did not have a shop sign
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: không có biển hiệu cửa hàng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: While he was working on a broken umbrella
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Trong khi ông ấy đang sửa một cái ô bị hỏng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Longer than you have been alive
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Lâu hơn cả tuổi của cậu
vứt những cái ô cũ đi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: his fingers still knew the work
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: những ngón tay ông vẫn còn nhớ công việc
Sau khi mùa mưa kết thúc
góc phố của ông trống không

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Relative clause with 'who'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Mệnh đề quan hệ với 'who' mô tả người: 'a man who repaired umbrellas' = 'một người đàn ông sửa ô dù'. 'Who' thay cho chủ ngữ là người trong mệnh đề phụ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: A relative clause with 'who' describes a person: 'a man who repaired umbrellas'. 'Who' replaces the human subject inside the relative clause.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Time clause: 'after' + past simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Mệnh đề thời gian với 'after' đứng trước mệnh đề chính: 'After the monsoon season ended, his corner was empty.' Cả hai vế đều dùng past simple.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: A time clause with 'after' can precede the main clause: 'After the monsoon season ended, his corner was empty.' Both clauses are in past simple.

sampleTranslationVi: Gần một góc phố yên tĩnh trong khu Phố Cổ Hà Nội, có một người đàn ông sửa ô dù. Tôi đi qua chỗ ông mỗi sáng trên đường đến lớp tiếng Việt. Ông ngồi trên một chiếc ghế đẩu gỗ nhỏ với hai cái hộp trước mặt: một đầy những nan kim loại bị cong và một đầy vải tối màu đã được gấp lại. Ông không có biển hiệu cửa hàng. Những người biết ông chỉ đơn giản đặt cái ô bị hỏng xuống đất bên cạnh ông và hỏi giá. Ông thường tính hai mươi nghìn đồng để thay một nan, và bốn mươi nghìn để khâu lại vải. Trong khi ông đang sửa một cái ô bị hỏng một buổi chiều, tôi hỏi ông đã làm công việc này bao lâu rồi. Ông cười và nói, 'Lâu hơn cả tuổi của cậu.' Hầu hết mọi người trong thành phố vứt những cái ô cũ đi và mua cái mới với giá bằng hai ly cà phê. Ông nói ông hiểu điều đó, nhưng không muốn làm việc khác, vì những ngón tay ông vẫn còn nhớ công việc. Sau khi mùa mưa kết thúc năm đó, góc phố của ông trống không. Một bà nhỏ bán hoa nói với tôi rằng ông đã chuyển ra ngoài thành phố sống với con gái.

mcQuestions.explanation: 'Who' is used for people; 'which' is for things.

options: He used to charged twenty thousand đồng.
He used to charge twenty thousand đồng.
He use to charge twenty thousand đồng.
He was used to charging twenty thousand đồng.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Used to + base verb' for a past habit.

options: to put in the rubbish, to get rid of
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Throw away' = dispose of, put in the bin.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While he worked on a broken umbrella, I asked him a question.
While he was working on a broken umbrella, I asked him a question.
While he was work on a broken umbrella, I asked him a question.
While he is working on a broken umbrella, I asked him a question.

mcQuestions.prompt: What is a 'spoke' in an umbrella?
options: one of the thin metal rods that hold the fabric open
mcQuestions.explanation: A 'spoke' is one of the thin rods that hold an umbrella open.
mcQuestions.hint: Long thin metal piece in the frame.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best comparative: 'Longer ___ you have been alive.'

options: the very hot dry summer season
a wet rainy season with strong winds
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Monsoon' refers to the rainy season in South and Southeast Asia.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural sentence: '__ the monsoon season ended, his corner was empty.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'After' fits the time order: the season ended first, then the corner was empty.
mcQuestions.hint: Order of events: rain ended, then he was gone.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'his fingers still knew the work' mean?
options: his hands had not forgotten the skill
his hands were tired and old
his hands could not feel anything
his hands could write the work down
mcQuestions.explanation: A poetic way of saying he still had the manual skill.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is best for explaining cost casually in English?
options: He charged the price of two coffees.
He charge the price of two coffees.
He charged the price of two coffee.
He was charging the price of two coffees.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple + plural 'coffees' for two servings is natural.
mcQuestions.hint: Past simple, plural for two drinks.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural phrase: 'on my way ___ a Vietnamese class.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'On my way to + place' is the standard collocation.

criticalThinkingQuestion: When old crafts like umbrella repair disappear, do we lose something more than just a small service? What is it that we lose?
